reviews.review_description: This is not a game, this is an immersive animated short. Enter knowing that and it’s very impressive. The visuals are gorgeous, with a very cozy intro rendered in a ‘best attempt’ to match Disney Theatrical style animation. The rest of the short is more stylized but no less immersive- I’m not too proud to admit I reached out to touch the Nokk when it ran by me! For a $3 experience? Definitely very good!

reviews.review_title: 5 Stars for an Immersive Clip-Not a Game
reviews.review_description: My daughter loved it and says, "It had a great performance but I didn't like that the screen darkens when you turn around."

Disney should be ashamed, Meta should be ashamed, and I should be ashamed for buying it (I am). This isn't even remotely worth it or any good even if it were free. There are soooo many better VR experiences out there! The good reviews are clearly fake. Do not waste your time or money like I did.

So surprised by the sour reviews. Wanting to see this VR experience spurred my purchase of the Oculus. It’s wonderful. The soundtrack/score is incredible, the visuals are stunning, and I love being immersed in the story’s world. I wish wish wish there were more apps like this on the Oculus.
I’m pretty sure you get like a free 5 dollars at the start of your headset but has like a week to be used and I was rushing and got this and it buns honestly buy something in a game like VRFS

reviews.review_title: This is an experience, not a game
reviews.review_description:
This is an experience, not a game. If you go in understanding that, it is great. 

This is a great introduction to VR. I usually do First Encounters and then this, for letting people experience VR.

I’m a huge Frozen fan, so I’m definitely biased here but I’m happy with my purchase. The experience is a couple minute long prologue to the second film explaining the creation of the 5 spirits we see in the film. The animation feels rough in places, like a choppy fireplace. I also wish it did not start fading to black if you look where you aren’t “supposed to”. I enjoyed the overall art direction though. I can see why people wish it were free but it’s still only $3. While you don’t see any characters from the film, seeing the earth giants and water knock in VR is easily worth that to me.
Nope
Just save you money it’s not worth it at all. It was like 5 minutes long and the story wasn’t even correct…

reviews.review_title: Don't waste your money!! NO CASTING
reviews.review_description:
1- You are not allowed to CAST. I feel this should be listed in the description.
2- You have to look in a certain direction to follow the story or to keep it playing
3- as you can imagine with children trying to watch, those two flaws listed above just dont work.

This shouldn't even be in the store if anything it should be over with the sidequest apps and it should be free.

Myth is a very interesting short film that you can buy in VR, but the question is whether you should spend the three dollar’s on this game.

Well for starters the game/short film is very artistic and has some good quality when it comes to the film with only about 15-18 minutes of viewing. (Side note) this short film does not contain any of the characters from frozen except for the rock giants, the water spirit horse, and the salamander. 

This short film explains the story of the spirits and how they all fell from harmony, if you didn’t catch on from the movie Frozen 2.

Now for the real reason why your here, why you should or shouldn’t purchase this short film, I believe this short film is really good but I do not think the film is worth the 3$, the short film would’ve done better if they decided to sell it for 1.50$ since it’s a reasonable price. If you really want to watch it just to get the experience I do recommend but if you don’t really have much money to spend, or not really interested in the story line I do not recommend purchasing this. 

(Side note) my little sister enjoyed this since she loves frozen, buy it for younger siblings or daughters/sons.
He is very in awe. I have to watch what he watches because he is autistic and, can be quickly overwhelmed. But he did very well with this.

So, as some of the reviews have never seen Myth on Disney plus. This is exactly what it is but in VR form.
It's 360 view from the short, Myth from Frozen 2.
This is just the "ledgend" of how the "5th Spirit" came to be if you don't k own what it is.

If you liked the short on the TV it was a cute experience to see it in VR.

reviews.review_title: Not Worth buying by any margin.
reviews.review_description: Seriously it is really bad.

reviews.review_title: I wasted $3 so you don’t have to
reviews.review_description: After reading many terrible reviews I decided to risk spending $3 to see for myself the reasoning behind it. For starters THIS IS NOT AN INTERACTIVE GAME ITS JUST A SHORT STORY. Does this have Ana and else in it? NO it does not. BUT is it frozen related? YES. it is, it’s MAINLY  about FROZEN  2 and the powers of the 4 rocks (air,fire,water and earth) it does have some of the characters from frozen 2, such as Elsa’s horse and the fire lizard. Overall for just $3 it’s not bad. I had no issues loading it either nothing crashed, everything loaded perfectly fine. I give it 5 stars only to even it out between the bad reviews but I’d probably give it only 3-4 stars because as I mentioned it’s just a more in depth story about the powers of the rocks. And if you’ve never seen frozen 2 then none of this will make sense to you.
Its ok for toddler or younger kid… so not entirely bad for $3.    But was definitely wasted on my 9 year old!
My daughter loves Frozen and sure enough loved this experience.

Small children can just watch it on Disney+. There’s no need for casting when you can watch it on a big screen. If you don’t want to pay for the VR experience you can watch it free on Disney+. This experience is for adults or older kids that can enjoy it. The bad reviews make no sense when complaining about those two things when kids can watch free already.

I first saw this with the extra features on disney plus with my daughter. I got it on the quest for her and she loves it. It's a great way to introduce those who love frozen into VR. My wife wasn't sure about VR but was sold when she saw this.
Short and worthless..
There is no story, no character from Frozen, and it's boring.

There are BETTER short films for free (Invasion and Henry for example). I am so disappointed. This should have been free. I expected more from Disney and my expectations were not met.

Biggest complaint is that if you turn your head everything starts to dim. You have to look into the right direction to follow the story and you are not allowed to look around freely. Which makes this more of an 180 than 360 experience.

I haven't shown this to the kids yet. I'm pretty sure they will find this scary.
The app keep crashing right past the logo. Didn’t even get to play try to get a refund but you can’t . Please do not waste your time or money. My little girl is very upset.

reviews.review_title: Only the first part was great
reviews.review_description: Only the first few minutes when you're in the room as you were in the movie were great. It would be nice to watch cartoons this way. The "forest" wasn't that much

reviews.review_title: I’m surprised Disney’s name is on this
reviews.review_description: I purchased this because I have a grandkid crazed about Frozen. Should have read reviews first. This was worse than what I would have expected. Graphics are very low rate and the story is very disjointed. I am going to return this.
If you want a preview of this app/video, they have it on Disney +. I thought it was pretty great! Stunning colors and visuals. Great for any Frozen 2 fan
Bought this for my daughter. And it's never worked. Loads yo and Sita at the main screen and does nothing else. Does anyone kno how to get it to start

Disney need to step up their game this is a mature hour in the vr world. 
DIMMING when you aren’t in the focal point they want is not acceptable they should just do a light arrow of some sort
I felt like I was in Disney, loved it! 😍
Love the art and experience of being in the environment. Nice job Disney.
Should be free, my kids were not impressed, won’t even allow casting to see what they see, disappointed.

Other reviewers are let down because they expect a massive production involving all the Frozen characters with environment interaction just because Disney’s name is on it.  Ok.  It’s a short that involves the element spirits from Frozen 2.  My little girl was excited to see the fire lizard and water horse “in person” really cool to see - and my boy was slightly frightened by the stone giants.  The whole family enjoyed it and is worth $3.   It is not dissimilar to the Jurassic World app that has the 8 minute story following Blue the velociraptor.  Similar idea. Yes I know that one is free, however it’s not narrated by a big name with a great musical score and all.  Bottom line is I don’t regret spending the $3 on it to see the awe and wonder on my kid’s faces.
I can see this being the beginning of an era for new fantasy content!

I think this is the way that I had hoped VR to be. Most of the content on the Quest requires you to sit still and simulate you moving around while you sit still. This gives new people headaches and nausea. You have to adapt to it. 

Here is a download that gives you a neat story and actually tracks your real time movements, removing the disparity. You can actually walk around in the movie!!!! The movie is truly happening around you like VR should be. 

Yes, it is short and yes there are things that could be added, but I think that this was more of a test. They probably wanted to test out something like this and review feedback and profits to see if it is worth moving in this direction. VR is finally starting to become more common, and so people are starting to put money into it, which will attract more progress in the things that could be added to future titles. 

I think for sort of a demo, this was incredible and I had to show everyone in the house. It's wild and the start of hopefully the way full length videos will eventually be. I really hope that enough people buy this demo and rate it well for what it is (A short trial for data - So ignore most of the complaints), so that Disney will continue in this direction. 

Thank you so much for this video. It is an exciting teaser for what could be the future.

reviews.review_title: Myth: A Frozen Tale from a Frozen fan
reviews.review_description: So, I read about this after watching it in Cinema and wanted to give it a try, but couldn't really find anyway, so when I saw it came out on the Oculus store I went out, bought a Quest and the film and was excited, and as a Frozen fan, this doesn't disappoint, sure it's short, but the 3$ price tag should have given that away, for me, it gives more context to Frozen 2's overall story, it expands on it and gives the viewer more knowledge about the spirits and how the Enchanted Forest works, (Spoiler alert for Frozen 2 here) It helps clarify the reason why Elsa stays in the Forest after the events of Frozen 2, and that ending was just beautiful, the lights and music almost made me cry happy tears. So yeah, from a Frozen fan POV, is it short? Yes. Would interactions be cool? Yeah, but I will say it this way, you buy this if you like or love Frozen 2 and want more to the world, don't buy this just because it's Disney, buy it if you wanna know more about Frozen 2's world.
Visually disappointing quite blurry and stuttering not as polished as the video makes out.
If you have kids it’s a must have....
Great experience and worth checking out for all frozen fans!
Who blocks chromecast on a kid’s app?
Boring, should have been free
It’s not as bad as people are suggesting, it’s a pretty experience. It could be longer and I wish Elsa was introduced but my kids liked it.

My 3
4 yr old daughter is obsessed and loves this brings frozen 2 to life and stationary
Could use a little more story but I love Britney’s art and it was very cute. Would love to see a series of these for other movies like Moana and the little mermaid.

reviews.review_title: Worth the money, here me out
reviews.review_description: My 7 and 5 year kids enjoy the video. To be honest, it was nice to pull something up that didn't require much brain power. They were about to enjoy the visual, but still have the interactive experience. Both kids asked to watch it multiple times. I wish there was more things like this
My little girl loved it, but it is so short, blink and u miss it! Graphics aren’t great either. Would be better if it was a bit longer and u saw Elsa

I think most of the reviews are being unfair. It’s a fun little Disney experience. It is very short and that’s a little disappointing. It’s also not that deep of a story and has a rushing feeling to it which is surprising for something Disney is putting out. But all said, it’s worth the $3, has ok visuals, really good soundtrack, and it’s still Frozen which is pretty cool. It just would’ve been nice is Disney sat on it a bit longer to do a little more work on it so it was a little longer. All said, pretty good and worth the price just to check it out.
Takes forever to run, if it ever does. Got it to work once.

Me and my girls enjoy frozen and this was a nice addition to the existing films. It's not as good as the "Frozen Fever" short film but it's still a nice experience, especially the horse which is a real highlight. 
Don't expect a huge amount from this, but if you are Frozen fans then this is a nice thing to watch for just a few quid.
It may be a bit basic, but... My daughter liked it, I watched it, it wasn't bad, could be a bit better resolution, for the money it's ok.

reviews.review_title: Too short. Emersion but no interaction.
reviews.review_description: You can't pause this experience which in my case I needed when my phone rang. well it's very pretty and it's very immersive in a artistic kind of way there really isn't any interaction which would have been much more enjoyable. As much as I like the Frozen franchise I would not recommend to purchase here.

reviews.review_title: Spend a few minutes in Disney Magic
reviews.review_description: Probably not a lot of replay value here, but when the water horse spirit walks in front of you it’s like being in a Disney movie. It shows some of the promise of VR but it is definitely over too quickly.
This was a cute little item. What you’d expect for $3.00
My 7 year old enjoyed it,  she's a Frozen fan.  It's like you're in the cartoon while they explain the 5 elements. The visuals are pretty. To me.... it was worth the $3's.
There's some amateur gaming content out there that's much better. Disney needs to step up their game. This was NOT good for a first impression for Disney.

Don’t know what ppl are expecting for paying this cheap. Honestly thought this was a great buy all in all. My cousins and friends love it and it’s a first step to a technology that’s just going mainstream. 100% worth it!!

I purchased and installed on my quest 2, reinstalled, and rebooted several times....the game just hangs up on the Disney logo and will not progress. This is complete garbage!!! I would like to think someone would at least try and see if the game actually worked before charging you money for it. Trying to look how to get a refund now.
Without casting I can’t be certain if my child is stuck or going to have an episode from fast movement or other light pattern.

I am new to vr and like other apps I invisioned it being interactive but I am glad it was not. I let my 4 year old use my headset which she badly wanted to be a part of the family playing and this the perfect amount of entertainment for her capabilities. She just watched in awe and I really enjoyed that she enjoyed it. Well worth the 2 bucks or 3 whatever it was for her to get to immerse herself into this and get to take part in use of the vr itself.

When I first bought it I was so excited cause I liked the movie a lot but it had none of the main characters and it honestly made no sense I hope that in the future they make something like this just actually good .
Considering the amazing things I've seen in vr already this from Disney is very disappointing.
